programmes.show_description: Fitness fan Rose feels her body's getting all the attention, and her love life's struggling. Basingstoke barista Clarissa seeks her dream woman. Will the naked pods bring joy?
Brand new series: As Friends and Family Week comes to an end, new information comes to light that threatens the future of several couples.
The Goggleboxers tune in to Gladiators, Is It Cake?, Casualty, The 1% Club, The Yorkshire Farm, This Morning, BBC News on the cost of living, and more.
Bill's Wedding: Beverly promises to throw Bill and Dolores a perfect wedding in less than 24 hours.
Dee-Vorced: Beverly is stunned to learn that Brea's mom Vicki is divorced. Barry struggles with feeling left out of the JTP.
The Obstacle Course One: Chris wants his bride to jump through hoops, and plans to turn his wedding into an obstacle course. But Charlotte dreams of a traditional family wedding.
The Prescott: Alex's new company puts her up at a luxury apartment with full amenities and famous neighbours - and the family are keen to make the most of it...
Legacy: Phil visits his dad to make sure he's OK after hearing some concerning news. Meanwhile, Jay gives Claire and Mitch a bunch of their old childhood memorabilia.
A short, cheeky clip from Rude Tube's naughty little sister, Rude(ish) Tube, featuring sports of the lawnmower, motorbike and bicycle variety.
Gordon Ramsay revisits four restaurants he tried to help: the Spanish Pavillion in New Jersey, and Kingston Café, La Frite and Capri in California.
Eracism: After seeing Spike Lee's Do the Right Thing, Adam attempts to end racism in Jenkintown. Geoff is horrified to learn that Erica has no moral compass.
Hanukkah on the Seas: Pop-Pop offers to pay for a family cruise. And during her first Hanukkah with the Schwartzes, Erica realises that they're not perfect.
Manny Get Your Gun: Everyone gathers at a restaurant to celebrate Manny's birthday, although Manny himself is experiencing a mini-life crisis about getting older.
Mother Tucker: Cameron loves his mum, so Mitchell isn't sure how to tell Cam that she makes him uncomfortable. When Haley breaks up with Dylan, Phil takes it harder than anyone.
Brand new series: As Friends and Family Week comes to an end, new information comes to light that threatens the future of several couples.
Louis, a male escort who's hairless from the neck down, meets Essex-born cleaning fanatic Fleur. Eccentric gas-fitter-come-art-gallerist Ian wants someone who'll love his quirks.
La Galleria 33 (Part 1/2): Gordon tries to help La Galleria 33, run by sisters Rita and Lisa, who blame the restaurant's failures on everything from tablecloths to the weather.
Black-Eyed, Please: Flanders loses his cool when his laid-back parents start to prefer Homer's company to his own. Lisa is bullied by her new substitute teacher (Tina Fey).
Dark Knight Court: When Bart's accused of playing a prank on the school marching band - a prank he says he didn't commit - Lisa must step in to defend her brother in court.
Boys' Night: Jay's attempt to avoid a symphony concert with Gloria and Manny lands him in the middle of Mitchell and Cameron's boys' night out.
The Musical Man: Cameron relishes being interim music director at Luke and Manny's school, but takes on the upcoming spring musical with a little too much zest and passion.
Brand new series: As Friends and Family Week comes to an end, new information comes to light that threatens the future of several couples.

programmes.show_name: New: Dance Moms: A New Era
programmes.show_description: Brand new series - Careful What You Wish For: Gina and Audrey vie for a coveted solo at Nationals. And some scandalous after-hours behaviour drives a mum and daughter away forever.
Friend with Benefit: When Lisa becomes friends with Harper, a new girl at school whose family are extremely wealthy, Homer befriends Harper's father.
Lisa with an 'S': Homer loses a large sum of money playing poker with Broadway legend Laney Fontaine, who takes Lisa under her wing and transforms her into a showbiz kid.
Boyz N the Highlands: Bart, Nelson, Dolph and secret bad boy Martin Prince go on a court-mandated wilderness trek as a punishment.
...Australia - Brand new series: At the fourth dinner party, one bride is determined to avoid any drama. Alas, a groom has heard some rumours and needs to confront her...
...Dinner Party - Brand new series: Joel, Juliette, Stephanie, Julia and Grayson dish the dirt on their MAFS experience, including vicious dinner party behaviour and a tense reunion. (Ep3)
This time, the Goggleboxers watch: Wildlife Rescue, Expedition X and Jamie's Air Fryer Meals. Also Baby Reindeer, Teen First Dates and The Circle.
Is there chemistry between hunky scientist Ashley and single parent Sonia? Burlesque dancer Becky meets budding comedian Lewis. Do Queen fans Gemma and Paolo have a kind of magic?
Can Naked Attraction help clumsy Stephanie, who never gets a second date, and first-time lesbian Bethany find their perfect matches?
Brand new series: At the fourth dinner party, one bride is determined to avoid any drama. Alas, a groom has heard some rumours and needs to confront her...
This time, the Goggleboxers watch: Wildlife Rescue, Expedition X and Jamie's Air Fryer Meals. Also Baby Reindeer, Teen First Dates and The Circle.
Eracism: After seeing Spike Lee's Do the Right Thing, Adam attempts to end racism in Jenkintown. Geoff is horrified to learn that Erica has no moral compass.
Hanukkah on the Seas: Pop-Pop offers to pay for a family cruise. And during her first Hanukkah with the Schwartzes, Erica realises that they're not perfect.
Legacy: Phil visits his dad to make sure he's OK after hearing some concerning news. Meanwhile, Jay gives Claire and Mitch a bunch of their old childhood memorabilia.
The One with All the Boxing: Former boxing champ Jordan plans a fight-night wedding in front of thousands, while his bride-to-be Danielle has dreams of a feminine and intimate day.
Dead on a Rival: Mitchell learns a valuable life lesson when Murray shows up unexpectedly. And the Dunphys' old neighbour Kenneth, who's now a tech titan, visits.
Paris: The family head to Paris so that Jay can accept a lifetime achievement award for his work in the closet industry. But his old nemesis, Earl Chambers, haunts him.
A short, cheeky clip featuring animals and food, from the always hilarious Tube that is Rude.
La Galleria 33 (Part 1/2): Gordon tries to help La Galleria 33, run by sisters Rita and Lisa, who blame the restaurant's failures on everything from tablecloths to the weather.
Bevy's Big Murder Mystery Party: Beverly's first murder mystery party takes a surprising turn. And Erica struggles to pick a major.
Cocoon: When Pops falls asleep at the cinema, Adam worries that his grandpa is slowing down. Bev drags Erica into a contract dispute with her cookbook publisher.
Dance Dance Revelation: Everyone gets in on the fun at Luke and Manny's first school dance. A trip to the mall with the boys becomes stressful for Jay and Phil.
Slow Down Your Neighbors: Claire becomes a determined neighbourhood vigilante hoping to catch and stop a menacing car that speeds through the streets.
E My Sports: When Bart starts earning money from video game competitions, Homer becomes his coach, and their team make it to the finals in South Korea.
Bart vs. Itchy & Scratchy: Bart's upset about a new, all-girl Itchy and Scratchy. Then he joins an all-girl pranking crew, Bossy Riot.
Girl's in the Band: Lisa joins an advanced youth orchestra in Capital City, while Homer works the night shift and nearly goes mad.
Will a gaming nerd finally find her 'geeky James Bond'? Can an actor overcome their First Dates stage nerves? And will an ex-wild thing's tattoos wreck his chances of new love?
La Galleria 33 (Part 2/2): The saga of Boston eatery La Galleria 33 comes to a dramatic conclusion as Gordon Ramsay gives restaurant owners and sisters Rita and Lisa an ultimatum.
Pulpit Friction: A trendy new reverend recruits Homer as a deacon. Dry cleaners accidentally swap Marge's wedding dress for one of Krusty's clown costumes.
The Fabulous Faker Boy: Bart doesn't want to go to piano lessons, until he meets his beautiful teacher... Homer loses the last two hairs on his head.
Someone to Watch Over Lily: Mitchell and Cameron secretly weigh up who in the family could be suitable legal guardians for Lily if anything ever happens to them.
Mother's Day: Claire and Gloria want to spend Mother's Day hiking with the kids, but their bickering and complaining drive Claire mad, and she brings Gloria down with her.
E My Sports: When Bart starts earning money from video game competitions, Homer becomes his coach, and their team make it to the finals in South Korea.
Bart vs. Itchy & Scratchy: Bart's upset about a new, all-girl Itchy and Scratchy. Then he joins an all-girl pranking crew, Bossy Riot.
Girl's in the Band: Lisa joins an advanced youth orchestra in Capital City, while Homer works the night shift and nearly goes mad.

programmes.show_name: New: Dance Moms: A New Era
programmes.show_description: Brand new series - Boop! There It Is!: The Big Apple plays host to some big drama at Nationals, with a soloist freezing under pressure and Kaeli reaching her breaking point.
Paths of Glory: Lisa sets out to restore the damaged reputation of Springfield's first female inventor. Bart convinces Marge and Homer that he has sociopathy.
Barthood: Bart's coming-of-age story is told, chronicling his life from youth to adulthood. But his tense relationship with Homer and being overshadowed by Lisa shape him.
You Won't Believe What This Episode Is About - Act Three Will Shock You!: Homer goes viral after accidentally locking Santa's Little Helper in the car.
Pray Anything: Jealous of Flanders' lifestyle, Homer starts to pray for material possessions, but things get out of hand when he asks for a bigger house and gets the church.
(2022) Danger-zone action as Tom Cruise returns to the cockpit after 36 years. He's Maverick. He still feels the need for speed. And there's a near-impossible mission to fly. With Jon Hamm.
Field Trip: It's a new term at Rudge Park comprehensive: time for the infamous sociology and geography field trip to Swanage...
Work Experience: Jay arranges work experience for himself and Simon at his dad's company, but a mix-up leaves Neil at a newspaper and Will at a garage.
Thirty-year-old Pablo is HIV positive and wants to break the stigma about his condition. Rapper Leon has been attracting the wrong sort of lady. Can Naked Attraction help?
